summary: Classified the live PR inventory shard without GitHub mutations. No shared canonical exists. The open PRs are useful but require maintainer or technical review because the job blocks merge/fix work and the hydrated artifact shows proof failures, dirty/unknown mergeability, failing checks, active contributor work, or unresolved review-bot concerns. Linked issues are kept open as their local canonical threads; the already closed context issue remains closed.

actions.reason: Keep the feature issue open as the local canonical thread for the cron calendar/timeline UI subfamily.
Maintainer/technical correctness judgment is required before this focused behavior change can move forward.
This broad feature PR needs maintainer review and technical correctness judgment; it is not safe to close or merge from this inventory shard.
Keep the P1 data-loss issue open as the local canonical thread until the linked fix PR is reviewed and landed.
The PR appears useful and credited to the contributor, but final technical/merge judgment must stay with a maintainer.
Closed context ref is historical evidence only.
Keep the feature issue open as the local canonical thread until PR #93523 is reviewed and validated.
Small useful PR, but failing/unstable checks and lack of review make this a maintainer decision rather than an automated close or merge.
Keep the issue open as the local canonical thread until the linked PR receives proof and technical review.
The fix needs proof and maintainer/technical correctness review before any close or merge path is safe.

needs_human: #41299: focused separator behavior change needs real behavior proof and technical correctness review before maintainers can decide whether to land it.
#41892: broad cron calendar/timeline feature is dirty, large, and has unresolved review/check risk; maintainer product and technical review is required.
#83933: useful P1 cron data-loss fix has contributor proof, but ClawSweeper explicitly asks for maintainer review and the hydrated checks include a failure.

#93535: one-file cron watchdog fix is plausible but lacks successful real behavior proof and has failing checks.
